items.title: Businesses expect softer outlook in Q2 2026: Statistics Department
items.summary: Mohd Uzir said the construction sector turned pessimistic, with the confidence indicator registering negative 9.5 per cent in Q2 2026 against 1.4 per cent in the previous quarter. Johor recorded a confidence indicator of 1.2 per cent, Kedah (3.2 per cent), Kelantan (3.8 per cent), Negeri Sembilan (1.9 per cent), Perak (0.9 per cent), Selangor (0.2 per cent), and Terengganu (5.5 per cent). A pessimitic lull is over Melaka with the confidence indicator at negative 9.1 per cent, Pahang (- 7.1 per cent), Penang (- 5.2 per cent), Perlis (- 7.1 per cent), Sabah (- 7.6 per cent), Sarawak (- 5.9 per cent), Kuala Lumpur (- 2.8 per cent), Labuan (- 9.5 per cent), and Putrajaya (- 2.3 per cent).

items.title: Storekeeper acquitted of trafficking over 3kg of drugs in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU: A vehicle storage facility storekeeper was today discharged and acquitted by the magistrate's court here of trafficking more than 3kg of syabu after the prosecution withdrew the charge against him. Magistrate Don Stiwin Malanjum made the ruling after the prosecution decided not to proceed with the charge under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 faced by Hadriansyah Sabran, 46. He said that after reviewing the representations and CCTV footage submitted by the defence, the state prosecution director decided to withdraw the trafficking charge.

items.title: UOA Development earnings drops 43pct to RM41.9mil in Q1 on lower revenue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UOA Development Bhd's net profit fell 43.3 per cent to RM41.94 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM73.95 million a year earlier, dragged by lower revenue and weaker fair value gains. Revenue declined 25.1 per cent to RM113.86 million, from RM152.1 million previously. On prospects, UOA said new property sales for the period ended March 31, 2026, totalled RM130.7 million, with Aethera Residences, Duo Tower, Bamboo Hills Residences, and Aster Hill as the main contributors.

items.title: Eight men, including six tourists, claim trial to possessing drugs in hotel room
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eight men, including six foreigners, pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court today to two counts of possessing ketamine and methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA) last Sunday. Malaysians Ivan Wong Yi Xian, 24, and Chong King Jun, 36, claimed trial after the charges were read to them before magistrate Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri. The six foreign defendants – Jensen Soh Kah Yi, 34; Lam Hoi Leung, 30; Hendry Gunawan, 28; Tang Waei, 41; Tran Quang Ha, 26; and Yeo Wei Seng, 37, also pleaded not guilty.

items.title: No roadblocks during Aidiladha - Loke
items.summary: He said the festive operation would involve integrated enforcement with police, focusing on accident-prone hotspots and inspections of commercial vehicles, particularly express buses. According to Loke, RTD personnel would conduct monitoring and inspections at bus depots and terminals before buses depart to ensure drivers and vehicles comply with safety requirements. He said enforcement officers would inspect drivers' licences, road tax, insurance coverage and Public Service Vehicle (PSV) licences, while random drug screening tests would also be conducted on bus drivers.

items.title: Penang, Johor, Selangor, Sarawak, KL account for 88.9pct of April exports - DOSM
items.summary: He said the increase was attributed to the higher exports in Penang (+RM33.5 billion), Johor (+RM9.7 billion), WP Kuala Lumpur (+RM3.5 billion), Selangor (+RM2.5 billion) and Sarawak (+RM490.1 million). Other state contributors include Perak (+RM472.2 million), Terengganu (+RM404.6 million), Sabah (+RM188.3 million), Perlis (+RM26.7 million) and Negri Sembilan (+RM25.2 million). However, exports decreased in Pahang by RM573.5 million, Labuan (-RM289.1 million), Kedah (-RM188.6 million), Melaka (-RM158.2 million) and Kelantan (-RM11.8 million).

items.title: Hong Leong Capital net profit drops 7.7pct to RM10mil in Q3
items.summary: Revenue fell three per cent to RM76.8 million from RM79 million. For the nine-month period, the company's net profit rose 14.6 per cent to RM46.76 million from RM40.8 million, while revenue climbed one per cent to RM256.9 million from RM254 million. Hong Leong Capital delivered an improved net profit growth of 15 per cent to RM46.8 million.

items.title: SMJ Energy rejects claims Sabah shortchanged in SAMUR deal
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah continues to receive consistent financial returns from its strategic equity participation in the Sabah Ammonia Urea (SAMUR) project. Through SMJ Energy Sdn Bhd, the Sabah government owns a 25 per cent equity interest in SAMUR, the company that owns and operates the SAMUR complex through a commercial collaboration agreement (CCA) with Petronas. Since the signing of the share purchase agreement on April 25, 2023, dividends have been flowing into Sabah's coffers through SMJ Energy Sdn Bhd.

items.title: France reports seven heatwave-linked deaths
items.summary: PARIS: The French government said Tuesday that seven people were reported to have died in connection to the recent heatwave baking much of western Europe, five of which were drownings. "What I can say today is that there have been seven deaths directly or indirectly related to the heat," government spokesperson Maud Bregeon told television broadcaster TF1. Bregeon's office told AFP that the drowning incidents occurred in different areas across France, from southeast Lyon to the Atlantic coast.

items.title: Former convict, groom-to-be claim trial to raping and molesting teenager
items.summary: Muhammad Haziq Aiman Bahrin, 26, and Muhammad Haikal Abdullah, 23, claimed trial after the charges were read out separately before judge Mohd Khairi Haron. Haziq, who was released early this year after serving a two-year prison sentence and whipping for rape, faced a new charge of physical sexual assault against the teenager. However, the victim was then allegedly molested by Haziq.

items.title: Balik kampung traffic builds up at Kelantan border ahead of Aidiladha
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: Traffic heading into Kelantan via Gua Musang has begun building up today as more balik kampung travellers returned home ahead of Hari Raya Aidiladha tomorrow, with congestion stretching about 4km at the state border. The congestion was concentrated along the Gua Musang-Kuala Lipis route near Kampung Mentara at the Pahang-Kelantan border, where vehicles slowed before entering Gua Musang town. "Traffic conditions remain manageable, particularly around Kampung Mentara, despite the increase in vehicle volume.

items.title: Anwar says korban is about sacrifice, resilience and helping the needy
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, May 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said the spirit of sacrifice should extend beyond religious obligations and serve as a reminder of perseverance, social responsibility and helping those in need. He said Malaysia has remained resilient despite economic uncertainty and global tensions. Anwar said worship should strengthen not only a person’s relationship with God but also with society.

items.title: Malaysia's ties with Iran helped secure fuel supply via Strait of Hormuz - Anwar
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia's strong diplomatic ties with Iran helped ensure the passage of the country's oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. Anwar said he is expected to receive a telephone call from the President of Iran at 6pm today, describing the communication as part of Malaysia's ongoing engagement with the country during a period of geopolitical uncertainty. The prime minister said Malaysia's continued engagement with Iran had also yielded practical benefits, particularly in safeguarding the country's fuel supply routes through the strategically important Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Snatch theft sentences: Why courts are rejecting lighter jail terms for robbery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Snatch theft and robbery warrant serious punishment because their impact extends far beyond monetary losses, crime analyst Datuk Shahul Hamid Abdul Rahim said. Backing the Court of Appeal's stance that offenders should face stiff penalties, he said such crimes instil fear and insecurity among the public, particularly women, senior citizens and other vulnerable groups. Victims, he said, often suffer not only the loss of valuables but also lasting psychological effects, with many becoming afraid to walk alone, use public transport or carry personal belongings in public.

items.title: Petrol supply stable until August, says Anwar
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, May 26 — Malaysia’s petrol supply is expected to remain stable until at least August despite global disruptions affecting energy routes and rising shipping costs, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. He said Malaysia’s fuel stability was supported by its international relations and continued access to key shipping routes, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz. Anwar said this had helped ensure continued fuel availability in Malaysia, including deliveries to Pengerang.

items.title: Son of Libya's Haftar vows to make up for 'lost years' under Gaddafi
items.summary: BELGACEM Haftar, son of eastern Libya's military chief Khalifa Haftar, touted in an interview with AFP what he called a push to make up for more than 40 "lost years" under slain ruler Muammar Gaddafi. After Gaddafi was killed during the 2011 revolution, Libya descended into civil war, splitting the country in half as the Haftar clan took power in the east. "Gaddafi governed Libya for 42 years, and he did not accomplish what we have in two years," Belgacem, 46, told AFP, seated in an office inside a lavish palace in Benghazi.

items.title: MACC cadet corps in schools key to early anti-graft education, says CJ
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh has proposed the establishment of Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) cadet corps in schools as part of efforts to instil anti-corruption values from a young age. He said early anti-corruption education should begin at the school level, similar to existing uniformed bodies such as police and army cadet corps. Meanwhile, Halim said the MACC has been carrying out preventive education programmes through its Community Education Division to spread anti-corruption messages among school and university students.

items.title: Enhanced radio network, air assets deployed in search for missing hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: TAPAH: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, entered its next phase today with enhanced communications support from the Government Integrated Radio Network (GIRN) at the Kuala Woh control post. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director (operations) Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the SAR operation is focusing on routes leading to Gunung Rayu and Pos Musoh, which are believed to be among the paths Jaslinda may have taken. Sabarodzi added that the primary control post remains at Kuala Woh, while the forward control post managing aircraft operations is stationed at the General Operations Force 3rd Battalion Camp in Bidor.

items.title: South Korea to step up monitoring of investments in overseas private debt
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea will step up monitoring investments by domestic pension funds and banks in overseas private debt, the country's financial watchdog said on Tuesday, as regulators worldwide become more concerned about private credit funds. The outstanding balance of private credit investments by Korean pension funds and other government-controlled retirement funds rose 55.3 per cent to 25.4 trillion won (US$16.86 billion) as of the end of February, from 16.3 trillion won in 2023, South Korea's Financial Supervisory Service (FSS) said in a statement. Local financial institutions, including brokerages, insurers and credit unions, had about 30.5 trillion won worth of exposure to private debt as of February, the FSS said.

items.title: Vape murder: Three brothers charged over fatal playground stabbing
items.summary: JASIN: Three brothers were charged in the magistrate's court here today with the murder of a 30-year-old man near a playground two weeks ago. According to the charge, the three men allegedly murdered Richard Edward near a playground along Jalan Anum 3, Taman Muhibbah, Merlimau, between 8pm and 8.30pm on May 13. Deputy public prosecutor Syazwani Yusri appeared for the prosecution, while lawyer L. Sherveen represented all three accused.

items.title: 'Quad' announces new Asia projects after questions on US
items.summary: NEW DELHI: The United States, India, Australia and Japan announced new maritime and critical minerals cooperation on Tuesday, reviving a forum viewed suspiciously by China following questions over the US commitment and disagreement on Iran. Meeting his counterparts, Rubio said the Quad comprised countries "who share strong values – strong, vibrant democracies – who also are committed to many of the same concepts with regards to economic development and have many aligned interests." Rubio said the Trump administration wanted the Quad to focus more on deliverables than meetings, and said cooperation was progressing "pretty aggressively."

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar assures Malaysians of sufficient fuel supply, says RON95 to remain at RM1.99
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has assured Malaysians that the country's fuel supply remains stable, with RON95 petrol to stay at RM1.99 per litre despite ongoing geopolitical tensions. Anwar said Malaysia was still receiving sufficient fuel supplies, including diesel, supported by strong diplomatic relations with countries in the region and the Middle East. Anwar said the government expected fuel supplies to remain sufficient at least through July.

items.title: One-way flights to Sabah, Sarawak capped at RM499 for Kaamatan, Gawai period
items.summary: KAJANG: The government has set a maximum price cap of RM499 for one-way economy class flight tickets from Peninsular Malaysia to Sabah and Sarawak for three days preceding the Kaamatan and Hari Gawai festivals. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the government will subsidise the difference between the actual market price set by airlines and the capped fare. Kaamatan is celebrated annually on May 30 and May 31 in Sabah, while Hari Gawai is celebrated on June 1 and June 2 in Sarawak.

items.title: Hong Leong Bank Q3 profit tops RM1bil on loan growth, stable margins
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Bank Bhd posted a net profit of RM1.02 billion for the third quarter ended March 31, 2026, compared with RM946.69 million a year earlier. Its quarterly revenue also rose 2.1 per cent to RM1.58 billion from RM1.54 billion, according to its Bursa Malaysia filing. For the nine-month period (9M FY26), Hong Leong Bank's net profit rose to RM3.28 billion from RM3.18 billion a year earlier, while revenue also increased to RM4.93 billion compared with RM4.77 billion previously.

items.title: Ex-national footballer, motivational speaker settle fines to reclaim seized luxury cars
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A former national footballer has retrieved his luxury Toyota Vellfire after settling all outstanding fines and offences that led to its seizure by the Road Transport Department (RTD). RTD deputy director-general (planning and operations) Datuk Jazmanie Shafawi had said that the former footballer's vehicle was impounded after officers flagged three distinct offences: driving without insurance, an expired road tax and an expired driving licence. "Our checks revealed that the vehicle's road tax had expired since Dec 7 last year.

items.title: Missing 19-year-old girl found dead near hiking trail after four-day search
items.summary: HULU SELANGOR, May 26 — A teenage girl who was believed to have lost her way while hiking at Bukit Changkat Asa here four days ago was found dead by rescuers today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the body of Nur Izzati Humaira Azizul, 19, was found off the hiking trail by the search and rescue (SAR) team at about 11.59 am. “It took the SAR team about 20 minutes to bring the body to the control post using a stretcher,” he told Bernama when met at the control post.

items.title: E&O FY26 profit jumps 31pct to RM221.2mil on record property sales
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eastern & Oriental Bhd (E&O) reported a net profit of RM62.05 million for the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q4 FY26), down 11.2 per cent from RM69.85 million a year ago. Revenue for the quarter slipped marginally to RM235.9 million from RM236.7 million previously. For the full financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26), E&O's net profit surged 31.2 per cent to RM221.2 million from RM168.6 million a year earlier, while revenue rose 17 per cent to RM867.6 million from RM741.1 million previously.

items.title: Amirudin replaces Nurul Izzah as PKR election director [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR has appointed its vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari as the party's election director alongside Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, replacing deputy president Nurul Izzah Anwar. PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said the appointment was part of the party's strategic restructuring to ensure its election machinery is further strengthened, better coordinated and implemented more comprehensively at all levels. Fuziah said PKR's new memberships reached 5,936 between April and May.

items.title: Hong Leong Financial Q3 net profit up 4.8pct to RM748.7mil on stronger divisional performance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd's net profit grew 4.8 per cent to RM748.7 million from RM714 million, supported by stronger contributions across all operating divisions. However, revenue slipped 0.6 per cent to RM1.69 billion from RM1.7 billion. For the nine-month period, the bank's net profit rose 3.7 per cent to RM2.49 billion from RM2.4 billion, while revenue climbed 1.8 per cent to RM5.55 billion from RM5.45 billion.

items.title: The palm oil peak is closing in sooner than we think
items.summary: For decades, palm oil has been the quiet workhorse of the global economy. No other crop comes close to yielding as much oil per hectare. When palm is expensive, soybean oil, sunflower oil, and rapeseed oil become expensive, too.

items.title: Man who stabbed woman in the neck ordered to undergo psychiatric evaluation [WATCH]
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A 32-year-old real estate agent pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to attempting to murder his female friend by stabbing her in the neck with a Rambo knife on May 17. According to the charge sheet, Ilhami is accused of attempting to murder Nurul Husna Kasmani, 35, by stabbing her in the neck in the parking lot of a restaurant in Titi Gajah, Kepala Batas, at 9.19pm on May 17. State prosecution director Datuk Khairul Anuar Abdul Halim, along with deputy public prosecutors Muhammad Fahmi Kamaluddin and Vatchira Wong Rui Fern, appeared for the prosecution while the accused was represented by Mohd Hakim Hamran.

items.title: DoE defends EIA process after transparency concerns
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Department of Environment (DoE) has defended its handling of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports following criticism by an environmental watchdog over transparency and public access to the documents. The DoE said that since January this year, with support from the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry (NRES), it had continued providing public access to executive summaries of First Schedule and Second Schedule EIA reports. The department said access to the reports would then be arranged by appointment at the DoE Library.

items.title: Kedah realtor charged with stabbing single mum in neck sent for mental health assessment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — A real estate agent accused of the attempted murder of a single mother in Alor Setar has been ordered to undergo a psychiatric evaluation at Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta in Tanjung Rambutan, Perak. Ilhamni Badaruddin, 32, pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court in Alor Setar today to a charge of attempted murder under Section 307(1) of the Penal Code, Sinar Harian reported. Judge N. Priscilla Hemamalini allowed Ilhamni to be referred to Hospital Bahagia following an application by his lawyer, Hakim Hamran.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia halts short selling in Critical Holdings
items.summary: The counter's share price dropped as much as 17.8 per cent to 83 sen in the morning trading session after recording lower earnings in the third quarter ended March 31, 2026. The stock opened 6.9 per cent lower at 94 sen versus its closing price of RM1.01 on Monday. It last traded 9.4 per cent or 9.5 sen lower at 91.5 sen, with more than five million shares traded.

items.title: Zahid takes Malaysian Bar’s DNAA challenge to Federal Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR May 26 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has applied for the Federal Court to set aside a Court of Appeal ruling that granted the Malaysian Bar leave to challenge the Attorney General’s (AG) decision to seek a discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA) for the Umno president in his Yayasan Akalbudi corruption case. Leave to appeal to the Federal Court is granted only if the case raises novel constitutional or legal questions of public importance being heard for the first time. Zahid and the AG are seeking to reinstate the June 27, 2024, High Court ruling that initially dismissed the Bar’s leave application.

items.title: PKR replaces Nurul Izzah as co-election director with Amirudin Shari
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — PKR has appointed vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari as its co-election director alongside Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, replacing deputy president Nurul Izzah Anwar. Nurul Izzah, the eldest daughter of PKR president and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, was appointed co-election director with Saifuddin in June 2025, a month after she defeated Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli for the deputy presidency. Fuziah also announced that Federal Territories PKR chief Azman Abidin has been appointed director of PKR’s election war room.

items.title: PM Anwar back in Permatang Pauh ahead of Aidiladha celebration
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim returned to his hometown in Penang today for a brief working visit ahead of the Aidiladha celebration. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, will present sacrificial cows to communities in the Permatang Pauh parliamentary and Seberang Jaya state constituencies at the Seberang Perai Tengah District Mosque near here at 2pm. The former Permatang Pauh MP will then return to his village to present sacrificial cows to the Permatang Pasir state constituency community at Masjid Al-Falah in Pengkalan Tambang.

items.title: Rollout of new Malaysian passport with additional security features postponed
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 26 — The Malaysian Immigration Department (JIM) has announced a postponement of the much-anticipated new version of the Malaysian International Passport (PMA), which was originally set to be introduced on 1 June 2026. The decision, according to JIM, is aimed at ensuring smooth service delivery and maintaining the best possible experience for the public when transacting for their travel documents. Importantly, the Immigration Department confirmed that members of the public may continue to renew their existing passports as usual.

items.title: Cable theft disrupts Northern Sector ETS and KTM Komuter services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — Several Electric Train Service (ETS) and Northern KTM Komuter services have been delayed by more than an hour following the theft of approximately 100 metres of cable at KM95.8, between Sungai Siput and Kamunting. In a statement today, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) said that the incident has crippled the signaling system, severely disrupting train operations in the northern sector. “KTMB’s auxiliary police detected the theft early this morning.

items.title: National security council says military exercises will follow strict safety, coordination measures
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 26 — Military exercises by the Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) will be carried out in close coordination with state governments and relevant agencies to ensure they are conducted in an orderly, safe and responsible manner, in line with national security interests and public well-being. The National Security Council (MKN) under the Prime Minister’s Department (JPM), in a statement today, said the MKN meeting on May 25 supported MAF’s commitment to ensuring all military activities are implemented professionally, systematically and responsibly. “The implementation of military exercises is subject to strict approval procedures and coordination with relevant ministries and agencies,” the statement said.

items.title: Yeoh: MACC finds no corruption in KL retention pond loss as Cabinet protection order was revoked in 2016
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) said it found no wrongdoing in the alienation of land around the capital city’s flood retention pond because a 1998 directive to protect it was revoked in 2016, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department Hannah Yeoh disclosed today. Yeoh previously urged MACC to investigate the land alienation in 2021. Yeoh said Federal Territories Department will expedite the gazetting process for the remaining 30 per cent of approved open spaces and flood retention ponds, expressing commitment to preserving open spaces and flood retention ponds.

items.title: Why is it still so hard to cut water losses? It’s the old pipes, says Air Selangor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — Malaysia has spent decades trying to reduce non-revenue water, or NRW, by chasing lower percentages and tighter targets. Air Selangor says that is where its challenge now lies, even after bringing its NRW rate down significantly over the years. Why cutting NRW gets harder According to SPAN, NRW remains a persistent challenge across Malaysia’s water sector.

items.title: Will MCA back Perikatan again? As GE16 nears, DAP leader dares Wee Ka Siong to come clean
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — DAP National Strategic Director Liew Chin Tong has challenged MCA’s Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong to be transparent with voters on whether his party would again back a PAS-dominated Perikatan Nasional (PN) to form a government after the next general election. In a statement issued today, Liew accused the MCA president of “sophistry” and “cunning deception” regarding the statutory declarations (SDs) signed following the 15th General Election (GE15) in November 2022. “The SD that Wee signed was to support Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, who was then chairman of Perikatan Nasional, in establishing a federal government with Bersatu and PAS,” Liew contended.

items.title: Breaking convention, Kedah MB keeps sacked Bersatu reps as exco and deputy Speaker
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — A Kedah assemblyman expelled by Parti Pribumi Bersatu (Bersatu) will keep his post as a state executive councillor (exco), while another lawmaking facing the same fate remains the Deputy Speaker of the Kedah State Assembly. Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor confirmed that Suka Menanti assemblyman Dzowahir Ab Ghani and Sungai Tiang assemblyman Abdul Razak Khamis would continue in their roles despite being expelled from Bersatu, The Star reported. Dzowahir serves as the State Agriculture, Plantation, and Transport Committee Chairman, while is the state’s Deputy Speaker.

items.title: Enforcement agency commission: Proposed ombudsman to enhance handling of public complaints
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 26 — The proposed establishment of a Malaysian Ombudsman is seen as a positive step towards ensuring that public complaints regarding government service delivery are handled more effectively, thereby enhancing the quality of public services. Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) chairman Tan Sri Dr Ismail Bakar said the Malaysian Ombudsman would serve as a platform for the public to lodge complaints on misconduct, maladministration or weaknesses in government service delivery, while also helping to transform public services to be more efficient and responsive to the needs of the people. “Insya-Allah, the Malaysian Ombudsman will begin operations next year once it is passed by Parliament.

items.title: Put it out: Kelantan health dept issues 85 notices for failure to display no-smoking signs
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 26 — The Kelantan Health Department has issued 85 notices of offence under Section 18 of the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852) against premise owners and occupants who failed to comply with no-smoking regulations between January and April this year. Its director Datuk Dr Mohd Azman Yacob said of the total, 43 notices involved food premises, followed by grocery stores (18), convenience store (1), and other premises (23 notices). He said the notices were issued following enforcement activities conducted on 6,209 premises statewide.

items.title: Holiday crush: Over three million vehicles expected on KLK, LPT1 highways during long break
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 26 — The operator of the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Highway (KLK) and the East Coast Expressway 1 (LPT1), AFA Prime Bhd (AFA Prime), estimates traffic volume to top 3 million vehicles on both highways from May 22 to June 2 due to Aidiladha and public holidays. Pahang Public Works, Transport and Health Committee chairman Datuk Razali Kassim said traffic on the KLK highway is expected to hit 2.18 million vehicles, while LPT1 is estimated to receive 843,000 vehicles during that period. Razali said the state government also urges the public to plan their journeys ahead of time and adhere to the Travel Time Advisory (TTA) issued by AFA Prime to help ease traffic flow throughout the festive period.

items.title: Impact injuries, drowning among causes of death in Sepat FSO tragedy
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 26 — Severe injuries caused by strong impact and drowning have been identified among the causes of death of three workers who perished after the lifeboat they were in plunged into the sea at the Sepat floating storage and offloading (FSO) facility, located about 150 metres off the Kuala Terengganu waters, at midday yesterday. Kuala Terengganu district police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said post-mortem results on the first victim found that Nik Muhammad Hafifi Asri Ab Majid, 38, died from serious abdominal injuries caused by a strong impact. “Muhammad Faezuan Hakim Mohammad Bustamam, 28, died from neck injuries, while Ahmad Fiqri Zakaria, 38, drowned,” he said when contacted.

items.title: Motorcyclist dies 13 days after being hit by driver believed to be under the influence
items.summary: KLANG, May 26 — The motorcyclist who was seriously injured after being struck by a vehicle driven by a man believed to have been under the influence of alcohol and travelling against the flow of traffic along Persiaran Kota on May 13 died yesterday evening after battling for his life for 13 days. Hamidah Othman, 51, said her son, Zulfiqar Zamir Amirul Nizam, 27, breathed his last at about 4.28 pm while receiving treatment in the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) of Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital (HTAR) in Klang. Meanwhile, the victim’s father, Amirul Nizam Abdul Aziz, 49, said the family would pursue civil legal action against the driver, who was alleged to have been travelling against the flow of traffic, following his son’s death, adding that a writ of summons had already been filed through their lawyer.

items.title: Two siblings who drowned in Paka laid to rest
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 26 — The remains of two siblings who drowned in Sungai Beril near Taman Beril in Paka, Terengganu, were laid to rest at the Kampung Buloh Poh Muslim Burial Grounds in Melor here yesterday. Muhammad Aidil Amsyar Mohd Zul-Zunaidi, 14, was buried at 8.20 pm, while his elder brother Muhammad Amir Ashraf, 15, was laid to rest at about 11 am today, with family members, relatives and villagers gathered at the burial grounds to pay their final respects. Muhammad Amir Ashraf’s body was found by the rescue team on the riverbed about seven metres from the location of the incident at 3.47 pm yesterday, while Muhammad Aidil Amsyar was found floating near the Paka Market jetty at 9.55 am today.

items.title: FT minister targets DBKL red tape to put KL on Asia’s concert map
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — Minister Hannah Yeoh has said that she is looking to slash bureaucratic red tape for concert approvals in the city here. Speaking at the launch of the KL Headline Season 2026 last night, the minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) said she intends to work with Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) and KL Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud to streamline current standard operating procedures (SOP). Yeoh said the government has already reduced deposits for concerts, local shows, and cultural performances.

items.title: Police nab Rames gang leader, alleged to be mastermind behind Kedah organised crime
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has cracked down on an organised crime network in Kedah following the arrest of the leader of Rames Gang who was a fugitive in a special operation recently. The suspect who masterminded the group and was active in violent crime activities in the Sungai Petani area was arrested on May 22 through Op Tiga conducted by Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID). Bukit Aman CID director Datuk M. Kumar said the arrest was made based on an arrest warrant issued by the Sungai Petani Magistrate’s Court on March 25 and a Red Notice issued by Interpol on April 24.

items.title: Sabah govt vows crackdown on fish bombing after reefs in Semporna damaged
items.summary: SEMPORNA, May 26 — Physical damage to the marine ecosystem at the Pulau Selakan Coral Reef Restoration Site here, caused by recent fish bombing activities, will invite firm legal action from the Sabah state government. Sabah Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said the incident, which destroyed an area of about 15 square metres, has disrupted coral reef conservation efforts carried out by various parties over the past two years. He said an assessment by the Tun Sakaran Marine Park Research Unit today confirmed that at least 10 reef star structures were affected, involving the destruction of key coral species including Acropora, Pocillopora and Montipora.

items.title: Kajang shoot-out: Police kill two alleged hardcore criminals in Bangi Lama
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — Two hardcore criminals were killed in a shoot-out with the police at Jalan Persiaran Seri Putra 2, Bandar Seri Putra, Bangi Lama, Kajang yesterday. Bukit Aman CID director Datuk M. Kumar said the shoot-out occurred at 9.10 pm. Kumar said a press conference on the incident will be held tomorrow.

items.title: Parents reminded that Article 15A citizenship applications now restricted to under-18s
items.summary: At the meeting, a total of 95 citizenship applications under Article 15A were presented, comprising illegitimate children (52 applications), children under guardianship (38) and adopted children (5). This brings the total number of Article 15A citizenship applications presented at monthly JKKSK meetings from 2024 to May 25, 2026, to 409 applications. The cumulative applications comprise illegitimate children (243), children under guardianship (136) and adopted children (30).

items.title: Going to Genting this long weekend? You'll need to pay RM5 toll starting Thursday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — Motorists heading to Genting Highlands will soon need to factor in a new road charge, with Lingkaran Cekap Sdn Bhd (LCSB) set to begin collecting fees on Jalan Genting Highlands from Thursday, May 28. LCSB, the authorised road operator for the popular highland route, announced the charges will be rolled out in two phases, covering entry points at Genting Sempah and the Gohtong Jaya roundabout. Motorcycles are fully exempt and need not register with LCSB.

items.title: Police raid upscale KL hotel, arrest 51 men for drug-fuelled immoral activities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — A total of 51 men were arrested in a series of four raids that uncovered drug abuse activities and a “gay party” at a prominent hotel in the capital early yesterday morning. Hussein said that during the raids at the scene, police also seized various types of drugs estimated to be worth RM103,070. This included 1.15 kilograms (kg) of liquid methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA), 284 grams of MDMA powder, ecstasy pills (8.56 grams), ecstasy powder (8.7 grams), and ketamine (11.61 grams).

items.title: As KL braces for concert surge, new ‘BTS’ initiative aims to curb ticket scams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has previewed Malaysia’s first national consumer protection campaign, the ‘Beli Tiket Selamat’ (BTS) initiative, which aims to curb event ticket fraud and scams. Sharing more about the initiative, Fahmi explained that BTS aims to protect Malaysians before, during, and after ticket fraud incidents through awareness, victim support, and structured intelligence sharing with enforcement agencies. At the centre of this initiative is belitiketselamat – Malaysia’s first dedicated platform for ticket scam awareness, victim reporting, and verified scam intelligence.

items.title: More than just a payslip deduction: What Perkeso actually does for Malaysian workers
items.summary: A worker earning the minimum wage of RM1,700 contributes RM8.50 a month to Perkeso, alongside RM3.40 to the Employment Insurance System (EIS), while employers contribute RM29.75 to Perkeso and another RM3.40 to the EIS. Centre for Future Labour Market Studies economist Zharif Luqman Hashim said the system exists to ensure workers have basic social protection when unexpected situations arise. Zharif noted that unlike private insurance, Perkeso operates as a compulsory social protection system where contributions are pooled and linked to wages rather than individual health risks.

items.title: Stagnant rents, mounting arrears force DBKL to spend more on PPR upkeep amid worsening public facilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 26 — Stagnant rental rates and rental arrears amounting to millions of ringgit have rendered public housing in Malaysia unsustainable, a report by Think City said recently. The report, titled “From Roof to Resilience”, pointed out that the Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) had maintained the rental rate at RM124 per month since the People’s Housing Projects (PPR) scheme was introduced nearly 20 years ago. Citing news reports, Think City’s report highlighted that DBKL spent RM164.08 million to sustain the management cost of public housing — more than seven times of its rental collection that stood at RM21.7 million.
